title: She’s running the Boston Marathon to support breast cancer patients
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Sydney Weisman, 24, from Fort Lauderdale, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support the Ellie Fund.
Weisman is motivated by her mother's breast cancer journey and the support received from the Ellie Fund organization.
The Ellie Fund provides assistance like transportation, meals, and childcare to families affected by breast cancer.

title: 3 Stop & Shop locations in Boston, Reading receive bomb threats, police say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston police responded to bomb threats at Stop & Shop locations in Jamaica Plain and Allston on Monday.
Reading police evacuated a Stop & Shop after a bomb threat at 25 Walkers Brook Drive with no credible threat found.
Authorities are investigating possible connections between the threats but have not confirmed if the incidents are related.

title: 4 things to know about new Patriots WR Romeo Doubs
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots sign wide receiver Romeo Doubs to four-year, $68 million contract with potential max value of $80 million.
Doubs recorded 55 catches for 724 yards and six touchdowns with the Packers in the 2025 season.
Known for playoff performances, Doubs has 20 catches for 371 yards and two touchdowns in four playoff games.

title: She’s running the Boston Marathon for suicide prevention after the loss of 2 friends
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Kerrie Jacoby, age 34 from Wellesley, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for suicide prevention.
She runs in honor of two friends, Tyler and Michelle, who died by suicide, aiming to raise mental health awareness.
Jacoby supports Samaritans and seeks to break the silence surrounding mental health struggles through her participation.

title: Southbridge woman dies after being rescued from house fire
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A woman in her 60s died after a house fire on South St., Southbridge, despite rescue efforts.
The fire showed from side windows with heavy smoke; backup was needed due to fire volume.
No audible fire alarms were found; the woman's death is the eighth in Massachusetts this year without them.

title: New ‘call a boomer’ phone line connects young people in Boston to seniors in Nevada
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Matter Neuroscience launched a 'call a boomer' phone line to connect Boston youth with seniors in Reno, Nevada.
The phone line near Boston University serves as a social experiment to bridge generational gaps through conversations.
Conversations from the phone line are recorded; clips may be shared on social media to inspire generational connection.

title: Gas prices keep climbing, and Massachusetts may see some of the biggest hikes
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Gas prices in Massachusetts have risen about 50 cents, reaching an average of $3.40 for regular gasoline.
Diesel prices increased from $4.04 to $4.83 per gallon, impacting delivery and consumer goods costs.
Massachusetts relies on imported fuel, making it vulnerable to global oil market disruptions caused by the Iran conflict.

title: Patriots reportedly agree to 4-year contract with WR Romeo Doubs
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Patriots sign Romeo Doubs to a four-year contract worth up to $80 million, per NFL reports.
Doubs had 55 catches for 724 yards and six touchdowns with the Packers in the 2025 season.
The signing follows the Patriots' release of Stefon Diggs, their recent 1,000-yard receiver.

title: Airbnb is offering $750 to new hosts in World Cup cities, including Boston
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Airbnb offers $750 bonus to new hosts in Boston area for World Cup 2026 accommodations.
More than 380,000 fans expected to use Airbnb, with demand up 80% in host cities including Boston.
Boston hosts could earn an average of $5,200, with matches held at Gillette Stadium from June 11 to July 19.

title: Wellesley native running the Boston Marathon to honor family with Alzheimer’s
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Colin Rowan, 53, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support the Alzheimer's Association MA/NH Chapter.
Rowan, originally from Wellesley, Mass., was inspired by his grandmother who suffered from dementia before passing away in 2011.
His 83-year-old mother is also experiencing dementia-related symptoms, motivating his effort to honor and support her.
